latest.title: Ducklings compare groups before they swim
latest.script_key: ducklings reveal the number sense first watch closely because young ducklings can compare simple amounts surprisingly early that number sense helps them stay near safer groups instead of wandering alone tiny feet useful math which baby animal should we test next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Los ríos ordenan sedimentos al doblarse

latest.title: Forests make cooler air under the canopy
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Solar flares aren’t what you think they look like
latest.script_key: solar flares aren t fireworks they re magnetic snaps like a rubber band breaking see the plasma curl back like a whip because plasma follows magnetic lines that s why they loop instead of explode outward watch this one twice the bright flash isn t the flare itself it s the shockwave

latest.title: rainbow between the mountains isn't where you think
latest.script_key: rainbow isn t where you see it look at the sky straight above the mountains that s just the reflection the real rainbow is 42 degrees away from the sun that s why it looks like it s sitting on the peaks you learned it wrong in school

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
latest.script_key: auroras start with charged particles from the sun watch the moving color because charged particles follow earth s magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere when they hit oxygen and nitrogen the air releases light the sky is reacting to space weather which rare sky event next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Mountains grow taller while you sleep
latest.script_key: that sunset rolling over the hills it s hiding a 24 7 rebuild glaciers carve valleys but the mountain itself gets taller because the crust below it bulges like a mattress bouncing back after you stand up that s why the alps grow half a millimeter every year did you learn this wrong too
